title: Gymnocalycium sutterianum
intro: Gymnocalycium sutterianum, also known as the Sutter's Live Yucca or Sutter's Pincushion Cactus, is a species of flowering plant in the cactus family, characterized by its short, stout stems and vibrant yellow or orange flowers. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Gymnocalycium sutterianum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, this plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote wound healing and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Gymnocalycium sutterianum is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Gymnocalycium, which is a group of small to medium-sized, globular cacti. The species is native to Mexico and is often referred to as a rare and beautiful addition to cactus collections. Gymnocalycium sutterianum is closely related to other species within the Gymnocalycium genus, such as Gymnocalycium stellatum and Gymnocalycium stellatum var. sutterianum. The classification of Gymnocalycium sutterianum reflects its unique characteristics and its place within the broader diversity of cacti.
uses: Gymnocalycium sutterianum is a species of cactus that has been used in traditional medicine due to its potential antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's stem and roots are rich in alkaloids, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that may contribute to its medicinal effects. Some studies suggest that extracts from Gymnocalycium sutterianum may have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which could be useful in treating various health conditions. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant. Further studies are required to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects.
